exercises.title: Comprehensive Stress Practice
exercises.instruction: Review all stress patterns: compound words, phrasal verbs, contrastive stress.
exercises.targetText: We'll set_up a follow_up meeting, but we need to focus on the action items.
tips: Phrasal verb 'focus on' stresses the particle: on
Stress: set, up, follow, up, need, focus, action, items

exercises.title: Comprehensive Linking Review
exercises.instruction: Review all linking patterns: consonant+vowel, same consonant, vowel+vowel.
exercises.targetText: Check_it_out and let_me_know what_you think about the results.
tips: 'let me know' - smooth flow between words
Stress: Check, let, know, think, results

exercises.title: Complex Thought Groups
exercises.instruction: Break this complex statement into clear thought groups.
exercises.targetText: Given the current market conditions, I recommend we proceed cautiously. However, if we wait too long, we might miss the opportunity. Therefore, I suggest we move forward with a balanced approach.
chunks: Given the current market conditions, I recommend we proceed cautiously.
However, if we wait too long, we might miss the opportunity.
Therefore, I suggest we move forward with a balanced approach.
tips: Use logical connectors to mark thought groups
Each complete thought is a chunk
Stress: market, conditions, recommend, proceed, cautiously, wait, miss, opportunity, suggest, move, forward, balanced, approach

exercises.title: Full Presentation Flow
exercises.targetText: Good morning everyone. Today I'll present our findings. Our research shows three key trends. First, customer engagement is up. Second, costs are down. Third, quality has improved. Based on these results, I recommend we expand the program. Thank you for your attention.
tips: Stress: Good, morning, everyone, Today, present, findings, research, shows, three, key, trends, First, engagement, Second, costs, Third, quality, improved, results, recommend, expand, program, Thank, attention

exercises.title: Comprehensive Intonation Review
exercises.instruction: Review all intonation patterns: statements, questions, lists, emphasis.
exercises.targetText: We have a clear plan. Are there any questions? We need commitment, resources, and time. This is critical!
tips: Statement: 'We have a clear plan' - falls at end ↘
Question: 'Are there any questions?' - rises at end ↗
Emphasis: 'This is critical!' - strong rise and fall with extra volume
